programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The murder of a bigamist leaves Mark wondering which of the victim's wives is the likely culprit - until he unearths evidence suggesting other parties may be involved. Guest starring Molly Cheek (American Pie)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve tries to solve the mystery surrounding the death of a wealthy woman residing in a hi-tech house. Mystery drama, starring Dick and Barry Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: When a sharp-eyed reporter spots a one-armed man in a police roundup of suspicious characters, she suggests in print that he might be connected to the Kimble case

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry is called in to investigate when a race car driver is charged with murdering the new business partner he felt was cheating him.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The hero faces a tough battle when he has to stop an Indian medicine man who is on a Hallowe'en killing spree

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate the fatal shooting of a mounted police officer - but the only evidence is a bullet lodged in his horse, presenting them with a dilemma. Aiden takes on the case of a dead youth discovered with severe facial burns, and soon finds herself trying to crack an illegal betting operation

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Hetty confronts Alexa Comescu and tells her Callen is dead, but the crime boss knows she is lying. Meanwhile, the agents search for their leader in Romania hoping to confirm she is alive, but when they mount a rescue attempt one of the team is shot. Real-time episode of the crime drama, starring Linda Hunt, Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Mickey Rooney guest stars as a comedian-turned-bookmaker who gets Kimble entangled in his troubles

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Police officer Dave Wolfe is charged with murder when the man who allegedly attacked his sister is found dead - and the bullets came from Wolfe's gun.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy Seal is shot dead while threatening to throw himself from the top of a building. An al-Qaeda operative emerges as the main suspect, but DiNozzo thinks the killer may actually be found closer to home. Elsewhere, a rumour spreads that Abby may be about to leave NCIS for good. Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star in the American drama

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker decides to avenge the murder of his parents

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the home of a bomb disposal expert who shot and killed an intruder during a robbery. The case appears to be straightforward until Tony discovers she lied about being alone during the incident, and the evidence suggests a link to a case in which a man walked free after paralysing a member of the public in a shootout. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The CEO of a software company linked to a cyber-attack against the Department of Defence is kidnapped, and Kensi and Deeks go undercover to protect the man's family after fears over their safety. Meanwhile, Callen continues to research his past

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The unit is called to examine the mysterious death of a sailor serving on a research vessel engaged in a top-secret mission. The ship proves to have been abandoned by its crew, and Ducky discovers the victim died from a highly contagious virus - putting everyone presently on board at risk. Drama, starring David McCallum, Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Lauren Holly

Guest starring Tracy Middendorf, Daniel Gillies and Kristen Ariza

programme.desc: In an effort to find out what life is like for Darryl behind bars, Tracey decides to build her own prison in the attic - with Sharon as her cellmate. Comedy, with Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A man is arrested for attempted murder, but claims he only hit out with a hammer to fight off two knife-wielding intruders and protect his daughters. While the team celebrate the result, by-the-book Sergeant Callum Stone is clearly not happy about his junior colleague's maverick methods

programme.desc: Malick's confrontational attitude prompts a female patient to make a formal complaint against him, and he is forbidden to operate on her. programme.title: Casualty

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's plans to celebrate his anniversary in style seem doomed when all his fellow vets want the same night off - and a goat makes matters worse by eating his cheque book. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: Lovejoy

Though convinced his luck must finally have changed, problems lie ahead when the item attracts the attention of a devious major. Tongue-in-cheek drama, with Ian McShane and guest star Leslie Phillips

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester decides to call in the experts to deal with redecorating - but her choice is less than ideal. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Following the death of a resident, Tom puts himself forward as a counsellor to aid and comfort the terminally ill. Meanwhile, Jane becomes confused when she overhears Tom and Diana's discussion over the existence of God. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy ropes Compo and Clegg into reconstructing a battle when a pit at a building site brings back memories of his wartime exploits. Comedy, starring Brain Wilde and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo, Clegg and Foggy are pleased when a stranger gives them an old van, but little do they realise what is inside it. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo Mills is persuaded to act on behalf of a drug addict, who went on the run 16 years previously after being charged with attempted murder. The defendant alleges detectives forced him to sign a confession and allowed him to escape custody - leaving Deed to decide if they should be charged with perverting the course of justice. Starring Martin Shaw, Donald Sinden, Jenny Seagrove, Caroline Langrishe and Robert Cavanah

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Sasha is occupied with a training course, the boys' investigations take them to a lookalike agency, where their top two acts were found dead in an apparent double suicide 13 years ago. In an industry reliant on the art of the illusion and their suspects intent on giving them the run around, Ted, Steve and Danny are convinced there's more to this case than first meets the eye. Steve is thrilled when their enquiries take them to the door of one of his football heroes, but his glee is short-lived. Detective drama, starring Denis Lawson

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The murder of Amir Aziz, a prominent anti-fundamentalist Muslim, drags the Lyell team into the murky world of hate-crime and counter-terrorism. Initially thought to be the work of a EDL member whose son was killed by the Taliban, the team begin to suspect the killing is connected to Sarah Begovic, a young woman who left the UK to relocate to Syria, but who recently returned home in secret with her six-month-old son. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring Richard Lintern

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A woman's corpse is discovered in a ploughed field, and the only lead is her husband, a potter. A year earlier, he suddenly decided to start a new life as a monk, suggesting that his wife may have committed suicide because she could not bear to lose him to the brethren - or that her spouse may have had a hand in her death. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.desc: James's plans for an evening out are spoiled when a dog goes missing after an operation. Siegfried struggles to keep the budget under control, and Tristan's love life creates more trouble. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard starts a panic in the neighbourhood when he discovers an outbreak of a lesser-known household pest. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, with Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo spots a sinister looking motorcyclist, and after recovering from the fright eventually concludes it could be one of his old girlfriends. Long-running comedy, starring Bill Owen

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A clubber is found dead from a suspected drug overdose - but the autopsy results reveal an even more disturbing truth. A preliminary investigation turns up a wealth of suspects, and brings Dalziel face to face with a ghost from his past. Detective drama, starring Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan and Bernard Cribbins

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: An ex-convict is found murdered in his home and suspicion immediately lands on a recently released child killer. Nikki and the Lyell team are introduced to forensic psychologist Sasha Blackburn, who works closely with those released from prison on licence, as both the victim and one of the suspects are clients of hers. The investigators soon learn that it is not just the minds of these young men that are fragile, but their freedom is too. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, guest starring Lyndsey Marshal and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A party of pilgrims is imprisoned within Shrewsbury Abbey on suspicion of murdering an old man whose body is discovered in a sack. Sleuthing monk Cadfael is soon on the scent of the killer, boiling the corpse down to its bones to ascertain the cause of death - and finding a tiny cross that could provide a vital clue. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Michael Culver, Julian Firth and Anthony Green

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey and Sharon witness a robbery committed by someone they know, leaving them agonising over whether to turn a blind eye or inform on a mate. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.desc: Maggie tries to hide her anguish as she tells her children they have the chance to go to Malaysia for a year with their father. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Emma and Sally are first to respond when two massive explosions rock Sun Hill. John and Gina monitor the shocking scenes of carnage from the station, while Callum takes charge on the ground, directing his team to deal with multiple casualties, confused and hysterical survivors and petty thieves taking advantage of the chaos. When it becomes clear a pervert has been posing as a doctor to molest injured women, Callum loses his grip and vents his fury on the man, but his troubles have only just begun

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy spends the day contemplating her future with Phil. Peggy and Grant have words after she sacks Lorraine, and Gita attempts to talk to Sanjay about a sensitive issue

programme.desc: Nigel is training for the London marathon. Bianca is planning her wedding. Ian threatens Ruth about her noisy tenants. Sanjay does not keep his appointment with Dr Legg

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan earns himself a reputation as the best vet in Darrowby, while James and Siegfried take a look at new technology. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer gets involved in a con at a country house involving the sale of a fake painting in exchange for a parcel of diamonds. However, Lovejoy later realises he too has been set up over the deal. Richard Griffiths guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma asks her parents to babysit while she and Peter attend a function in London. Meanwhile, Hester delivers meals on wheels to one of Sonia's old ladies but gets lost, collides with a policeman and causes a traffic jam. comedy, starring Julia MaKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: New resident Daisy tries to instil some discipline, but Diana is worried about her intentions toward Tom. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Alcohol threatens to prevent Yorkshire's first trainee astronaut from doing his job, so Foggy prepares to step into his boots. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After seeing Howard fail in his attempts at hypnotising Pearl, Foggy becomes convinced he's a master at the art. However, his attempts to practise on strangers backfire in spectacular fashion. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth goes to North Yorkshire to meet her long-lost cousin Kate, and ends up investigating a case of demonic visitations. Amiable Geoffrey joins her in an attempt to provide moral support - and is soon drawn into his own little mystery

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Gene investigates the shooting of a Ugandan Asian man and jumps to the conclusion that the attack is drug-related when he finds a suspicious bag of powder on the victim. When the substance turns out to be heroin, the policeman becomes determined to keep it off the streets of Manchester - and decides to do whatever it takes to get his way. Fantasy drama, starring Philip Glenister and John Simm

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A skeleton uncovered in the foundations of a swimming pool is identified as a young lottery winner named Cheryl, who was reported missing 17 years ago. The prime suspects are her fellow quiz team-mates, who won their lucky windfall as part of a syndicate, and while some have put their money to good use, the victim's boyfriend has fallen on hard times and the media remain convinced he is the key to her murder. As allegations of affairs, fights and betrayal begin to surface, the team is left to ponder whether money really is the root of all evil

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The accidental fatal shooting of a Polish teenager in an armed police operation sparks a series of murders, which links Turkish gangsters, a grieving family and a fractured firearms unit. As the inquest delivers a lawful killings verdict, IPCC investigator Vicky Sharpe calls on the Lyell team to investigate the connection between the events and review the police evidence, but Sharpe's relationship with the family of the teenager and her suspicion of the firearms division lead Nikki and Thomas to distrust her motives. Feature-length episode, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: When the medieval monk investigates the murder of a wealthy cleric, the finger of suspicion immediately points to a new arrival at the abbey, but as Cadfael digs deeper, he uncovers connections to a family with tangled loyalties. Drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Terence Hardiman, Michael Culver, Ian McNeice, Christien Anholt and Julian Glover

As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover is asked to rescue a pastel portrait of a young couple damaged by water, while clockwork maestro Steve Fletcher gets to work on a 1950s toy rocket which used to make sparks. Finally, leather expert Suzie Fletcher works on a cherished purse that has seen better days, crumbling and being held together with elastic bands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Women's Equality Party candidate for mayor of London

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Three new sellers arrive hoping to persuade the dealers to bid against each other for a child's penny farthing, two unusual optician's mirrors and an antique ice shaver

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Sudden Death: My Sister's Silent Killer
programme.desc: A man sets out to learn more about the death of his 19-year-old sister from Sudden Adult Death Syndrome, a cause of cardiac death even the experts don't completely understand. In seeking answers, Patrick is shocked to discover the true extent of the problem, and that his sister Lauren was one of around 600 young and seemingly healthy people who die suddenly and unexpectedly from a Sudden Cardiac Death each year. He attends a bereavement counselling session with other young people who have lost a sibling to SADS, meets leading cardiac-pathologist Professor Mary Sheppard, and talks to former footballer Fabrice Muamba, who famously survived SADS after collapsing during a match

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam knows his vlog has little chance of going viral with such a small audience, so he visits several successful influencers in an attempt to identify his own appeal. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam must use his new-found status as a micro-influencer to become the face of an ethical campaign but ends up fronting an advert for an oil company. Meanwhile his vlogger pal Charlie South is being 'un-cancelled'.

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits the village of Send in Surrey to see a two-up two-down property with potential to extend. Martin Roberts is in Derby to visit a three-bed semi that he feels needs opening up to make the most of its space, while Martel Maxwell is in Co Durham in the village of Blackhill Colliery to see a two-bed end-of-terrace with a very low sounding guide price. All properties are auctioned and the hosts talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves to be the undoing of a con man claiming he cannot work due to an injury, and in America, a doctor abuses his position to commit medical health insurance fraud

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, experts Philip Serrell and Raj Bisram guide two teams around the Harrogate Fair, seeking keenly priced items to take to auction. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors progress and also stops off at one of best-preserved Victorian Turkish baths in the UK. Later at the auction, Caroline Hawley takes to the rostrum to wield the gavel

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and green-fingered siblings Harry and David Rich travel to Milton Keynes to compete for the chance to create a garden for husband and wife Vinay and Raunak. Vinay loves to host barbecues, so he's after a garden where he can do all that in style. Raunak, meanwhile, is more interested in filling the garden with vivid and dramatic colours, to match the style of decor she has used throughout their house. Both want the garden to represent the Hindu religion's belief in the power and importance of nature

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps an aspiring writer who wants to make a dramatic relocation from the frenetic bustle of east London to the rural idylls of Dumfries and Galloway in Scotland. With a budget of £300,000, he has his sights set on somewhere isolated that will inspire his creativity. Away from the houses, Alistair heads for the hills hoping to strike it lucky on a Scottish gold rush

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: As busy mothers, friends Lucy and Hannah want quick delicious dishes they can cook for all the family. They are in the right place today as chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis are both wizards in the kitchen and masters of fabulous food against the clock. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture experts, horologists, metal workers, ceramicists and upholsterers try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glory

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: An election broadcast by a candidate for mayor of London

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the live magazine show, featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The time comes for Kush to face the music at his plea hearing, and as he arrives at court he is anxious to see Ben, reminding him of what's at stake should he say the wrong thing. Sheree is exasperated with Patrick when he insists he wants to go ahead with the stroke trial. Tiffany and Dotty are amused at Keegan's popularity as he serves a long line of excited customers on his market stall

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A Scottish dog lover showcases a range of natural doggie treats, hoping Dragons Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies and Touker Suleyman will throw her a financial bone. Others braving the heat of the Den include a beauty entrepreneur with her range of affordable-yet-luxury waterless face oils and a Northumbrian inventor hoping to demonstrate his nifty construction bracket. Plus, a tech entrepreneur is put through her paces when she showcases fitness app aimed solely at women. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by reality star Spencer Matthews and model Vogue Williams, aiming to identify the real Mitch from a line-up of four. Mitch is a interiors enthusiast, who has lovingly created a green-heavy home in Hinckley near Leicester. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs a topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the virtual audience

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Documentary following the climate activist over the course of 2019 and 2020, as she travels the world exploring the science of global warming and challenging world leaders to take action on the growing crisis. The programme follows the impact of Covid-19 on her campaign, and the challenges of persuading a world living through one crisis to face up to the possibility of another on the horizon. In the first programme, Greta travels through North America on her way to a UN climate conference in Chile

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts reports from a derelict semi in Dartford, Kent, while Martel falls in love with a characterful terrace in Stoke-on-Trent. Lucy Alexander is at an old water booster station in Buckinghamshire, where there are grand plans to convert it into a family home

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A holiday insurance scam is foiled when investigators uncover forged documents, while CCTV footage exposes the false claims of a man who said he was hit by a bus

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, Sarah and Stewart are called to help a lady who has fallen at home, while in Glasgow, crew members rush to a woman struggling with breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques Fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets the lowdown on the resurgence of brown furniture, before the whole team heads to Derby for some auction action

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Fortunas of Bolton invite Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers to revamp their garden in Bolton. The final result needs to have lots to entertain the energetic boys, but that is not the only requirement - Luciano is from Sicily and would love a garden that reminds him of home, complete with a pizza oven

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Bedfordshire with a sociable couple who have £500,000 to find the right country home to suit them and their pooches and to house their very own bespoke garden pub. Aside from houses, Margherita discovers the county's agricultural pedigree when she encounters some wonderfully preserved vintage tractors

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Cousins Joe and Alex, who have both experienced sight-loss problems, compete in the culinary contest. They both love to cook, and Alex inparticular has had to adjust to cooking by using some nifty kitchen gadgets, which he demonstrates to host Rylan Clark-Neal. The duo are paired up with chefs Mike Reid and Romy Gill

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Wood expert Will Kirk works wonders on an ornate, wired bird cage gifted to a daughter by the biological father she only managed to track down as an adult. Plus, leather expert Suzie Fletcher wrestles a beaten-up boxing bag back into shape

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. Guests including Richard Osman, Claudia Winkleman, Samantha Morton, AJ Odudu, Miles Jupp, Laura Whitmore, Lou Sanders, Chris McCausland, Sara Barron, Raj Bisram, Bob Mortimer, Sara Pascoe, Les Dennis, Mr Motivator, Stephen Hendry, Sindhu Vee, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Horne join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush takes drastic action, Martin faces a big decision and Lola confronts Isaac to ask why he took Patrick's phone. Tiffany overhears two girls talking about Keegan in the bar, having seen him online - but her confidence is shattered when they criticise his wife, prompting her to make a big blunder that leaves her in hot water

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and director Adrian Dunbar hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Katherine Ryan and Sunday Times journalist Tim Shipman joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: True Story (2015)
programme.desc: Disgraced New York Times journalist Michael Finkel discovers that Christian Longo, a man accused of murdering his family, has assumed his identity. Iintrigued, the reporter begins meeting with Longo, though as time goes on realises how dangerous his doppelganger really is. Jonah Hill, James Franco and Felicity Jones star in this thriller, based on Finkel's chilling, real-life story

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Shrewsbury, where Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss guide the red and blue teams. Christina also finds out about Tudor architecture

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies assess more pitches. A pair of tea entrepreneurs find themselves in hot water when their numbers are interrogated, the Dragons discover hidden artistic talents when a mindfulness art business is pitched, an optical inventor hopes to have investment in sight with his anti-glare eyewear and a husband-and-wife team have the needs of NHS workers at the heart of their skin-care business. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves.

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Cardiff - a quirky terraced home, a modern renovated bath-house and a new build that was shortlisted for the Welsh Architecture Prize

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After his eventful evening at Woody and Tracy's wedding, George tries once and for all to sort out his love life without upsetting anyone. Erik Thomson stars

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Kate Humble, Patrick Ayree, scientists and wildlife cameramen examine the changing seasons at Yellowstone National Park during 2016, and the effect on the local American wildlife. In the first episode, Patrick follows the grizzly bears, who are taking a risk with the weather by leaving their winter dens early. Meanwhile, Kate gets to grips with the science behind this frozen season

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall concludes his look at the planet's most fascinating ecosystems with a look at the Arctic archipelago of Svalbard. He reveals that it is home to an array of wildlife including the world's largest land predator, and uncovers a surprising secret about the remote region

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Adam Partridge appraise collectibles at Chiddingstone Castle in Kent. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin visits Brands Hatch and experiences what's made this track a huge success for 100 years

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A grandfather clock that has been in a family for over 100 years and a drum kit with a very special connection are among the transformations by the skilled team of crafters

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The first of the Wales heats sees Hywel Griffith, Chris Cleghorn, Nathan Davies and Ali Borer prepare their starters, with one chef leaving at the end of the programme. Which one that is will be decided by the veteran judge of this week's competition, a previous winner who remains a surprise to the contenders until the moment they walk into the kitchen to taste the first course

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a three-bed home in Surrey, which has a poorly positioned front door and a cluttered hallway. The owners are divided over whether an extension would solve their problems, and the architects present them with two very different visions for what their home could be. Laura takes a characteristically pragmatic approach with a simple but striking contemporary design. But experimental architect Robert decides a far more radical approach is the only answer

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: Documentary examining corruption in the police in the 1970s, revealing a secret network of officers was operating illegally throughout London and led to the formation of the first internal anti-corruption unit A10, which inspired the BBC drama Line of Duty. The story begins in 1969, when a desperate south London criminal tipped off the Times about how he was being extorted for money by a detective in the Metropolitan Police

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Richard Osman, Lolly Adefope and Lee Mack join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'naked truth' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The presenter returns to Springfield Hospital to work with the team and meet patients in crisis once again as they battle mental health issues during the Covid-19 pandemic. She meets patients whose conditions have been exacerbated by the challenges of lockdown, including two people struggling with psychosis and someone who is trying to control the daily rituals that govern her life

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals continue as the second group of surviving amateurs cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace that they would have on the menu of their own restaurant, pop-up or featured in their cookbook. In an unexpected twist, the contestants discover that they are also presenting their food to top chef Marcus Wareing. After two leave the contest, the remaining three head to The Game Bird restaurant at The Stafford Hotel to work with culinary director Ben Tish, serving the restaurant's staff. No one knows the food better than they do and expectations are high of the contenders

programme.title: My Father and Me (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary maker Nick Broomfield takes a distinctly personal look at his relationship with his humanist-pacifist father Maurice, a factory worker turned photographer of vivid images of postwar England. These images inspired Nick's own film-making career, but also spoke to a difference in outlook between the pair

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development of a commercial property in Ebbw Vale in Blaenau Gwent, as well as semi-detached houses in Lower Gornal in the West Midlands and Gillingham in Kent

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The naturalist looks at deception, theft and cheating in the natural world, revealing which species are the leading fraudsters and how they fool others into getting what they want. Chris shows how sea otters kidnap pups and hold them to ransom for food, while white-winged choughs pressgang youngsters from another family into their own so they get extra help to feed their chicks

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Yasmin, Miki and Andrew must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to Cardiff to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into each before deciding which one they like the most. Narrated by Marcus Garve

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George and Katie tackle the difficult issue of taking their burgeoning relationship to the next level, while Arlo is still struggling to work things out with Lindsey and Poppy. Elsewhere, Shay is led astray by Siouxsie, and Big Mac launches a plan to entice more tourists to Weld. Erik Thomson stars

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Cameras follow as winter turns to spring in Yellowstone during 2016, capturing the re-emergence of a range of animals who leave their winter dens behind to explore the new, green world. Kate Humble and Patrick Aryee witness young bison taking their first steps, beavers feasting on fresh green shoots, and grizzly bear cubs leaving the den for the first time

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the ecosystem of Canada's coastal forests, home to some of the largest trees on Earth and supporting a huge numbers of predators. He assesses the relationships between the seasons, the landscape and the wildlife to discover what might be fuelling this ancient environment's productivity and sustaining animals such as eagles, bears and wolves

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques at Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, as experts Christina Trevanion and Anita Harper take a look at the collectibles brought in by the public. Paul also takes a stroll down the high street of Amersham to explore the market town's history

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: An inherited 1950s moped and a hand-cranked butter churn barrel that brings back fond memories are among the items transformed. Sabrina Grant hosts

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. In the first edition, Fred heads to Venice in the company of chef-restaurateur Angela Hartnett whose Italian family heritage has given her a lifelong love of Italian food. She takes him enjoy the simple but stunning food at her favourite restaurant Osteria Alle Testiere and the unforgettable service at Caffè Florian - one of the oldest cafes in Europe

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three remaining chefs are half way through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating British invention and innovation. For mains, one chef has a lamb dish in honour of Welshman David Thomas, who revolutionised the steel industry in south Wales with his hot blast furnace. Another pays homage to Welsh entrepreneur Sir Pryce Pryce Jones, who invented mail order service

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: As the pandemic continues, people are increasingly looking for someone to blame, and asking whether life can ever return to normal. Covid-19 deniers become more numerous and vocal, as alternative and conspiratorial thought battles conventional science-based reason

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones and Thanyia Moore. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton visits the remote Treshnish Islands, where he experiences a spot of puffin therapy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Katie Razzall

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: Documentary examining corruption in the police in the 1970s, revealing a secret network of officers was operating illegally throughout London and led to the formation of the first internal anti-corruption unit A10, which inspired the BBC drama Line of Duty. The story begins in 1969, when a desperate south London criminal tipped off the Times about how he was being extorted for money by a detective in the Metropolitan Police

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary explores why it has taken so long for the full scale of child abuse in football to emerge. The documentary follows the investigation into former Southampton youth coach Bob Higgins, filming alongside Hampshire Police detectives as they conduct interviews with Higgins and the survivors

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last challenge of the semi-finals sees the six remaining amateurs cook-off to win a coveted place in the MasterChef Finals. They are tasked to each cook an outstanding dish to impress revered restaurant critics Grace Dent, Jay Rayner and Jimi Famurewa. Serving food to these masters of the dining room is a daunting task for the most adept of chefs, and these talented home-cooks now have to put their skills and palates on the line. Any mistake could cost them the competition, so the pressure is firmly on. It remains to be seen who John Torode and Gregg Wallace will take through to the prestigious finals week

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Returning home from South Africa, the latest potential buyer has a budget of £450,000 and heads west to Cornwall, with Steve Brown leading the search for a character property that has holiday let potential. Steve also pays a visit to the Newlyn Copper Works and has a bash at transforming the striking reddish metal into a delicate work of art

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First to arrive at the barn are sisters Christine Hopkins and Pauline Samways from Kidderminster, who bring in a miniature bicycle built by their grandfather. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West takes receipt of a small mosaic crucifix with an extraordinary story, and Steve Fletcher turns his attention to an army issued box kite. Finally, antique toy enthusiast David Burville sets to work restoring a Victorian acrobat toy

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Aaron, Sarah and Yasmin must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Elizabeth and Sean need builders to repair a DIY bodge job on their fireplace in Stretford, while first-time buyer Sam wants his tired kitchen in Battersea transforming

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George agrees to go camping with Katie, but their ideas of what that entails are very different and they end up being followed on the trip by half the people they know

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: The arrival of summer in Yellowstone brings a new challenge for the beavers as their river dries up, forcing the colony to move. As food becomes scarce, the wolves have a surprising strategy to keep their pups fed, while grizzly bears pay an unexpected visit to a cowboy ranch. By midsummer, the hot and dry conditions create a new danger as deadly wildfires threaten to engulf a family of great grey owls. Presented by Kate Humble and Patrick Aryee

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall dives beneath the surface of Australia's Great Barrier Reef to examine the conditions that allowed a tiny coral block to create the largest living structure on the planet. Plus, he discovers how it continues to flourish despite the coast's storms and nutrient-poor water

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey value antiques at Southwell Minster, near Nottingham, and presenter Paul Martin finds out how the minster has had a cavalcade of historical figures pass through it, from Cardinal Wolsey to Charles I

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A vintage car - once a father-and-son project but now rusting away - and a cake stand with huge sentimental value are among the objects transformed into functional items

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the nation's lochs, discovering the secrets of these iconic features of the Scottish landscape

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Wales must go head to head and cook their six-course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice? They serve a panel of judges and guest judge Hugh Hunt from the world of maths and engineering, whose skills are used to work out how to do daredevil stunts on TV

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the cottage garden at Longmeadow, Monty Don offers advice on what to plant under shrubs and roses. On a visit to Dunsborough Park in Surrey, Carol Klein celebrates a spectacular display of tulips, and Kate Bradbury goes through the first stages of creating a wildlife garden in Brighton

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Kelly Osbourne, Ade Edmondson, Sara Pascoe, Jermaine Jenas and Nicola Coughlan play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Faisal Islam

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Cally is horrified when she discovers the truth about the four Cylons on board Galactica, and takes a drastic course of action. Meanwhile, Lee's political career gets off to an uncertain start, the divisions within the Cylon fleet lead to violence and Boomer suffers from divided loyalties

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Gaius Baltar begins to openly promote belief in the Cylon god, setting off a debate on religious freedom. Colonel Tigh, Galen Tyrol, and Tory Foster argue over how far they should go to ensure their secret is not revealed. Sci-fi adventure, starring James Callis, Michael Hogan, Aaron Douglas and Rekha Sharma

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: The story of the first year of the pandemic told by people from all over the world. It begins on New Year's Eve 2019, when huge crowds gathered to celebrate. Contributors include Xie, a young food blogger from Wuhan whose wedding took place a few days before her city went into lockdown. As the virus travels to Europe, Dr Amie from Coventry recalls watching in disbelief the horrific scenes of people dying in Italy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best continues to explore lost railway routes in Northern Ireland, visiting lines that ran through towns and villages around Lough Neagh

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross captures a mountain at the height of winter, beneath one of his trademark cool blue skies

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: Five-part documentary about the scandal that toppled a president, beginning with the 1972 break-in at the Democratic Party's headquarters at the Watergate Hotel

programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: The documentary continues with Richard Nixon's efforts to cover his tracks following the break-in at the Democrat headquarters

programme.title: Lights Up Festival
programme.desc: A young Congolese woman begins to tell her story online - words of survival and solidarity - despite growing up in a culture that insists these words must never be spoken. Drama, written and performed by Dorcas Sebuyange

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer Zoe Laughlin examines the toothbrush, an item so ubiquitous that most people do not give it a moment's thought - and yet 200 million of them are thrown away each year. Zoe sets out to create a sustainable solution, heading to a low-impact factory in Sweden where handles are made from sugarcane bio-based plastic and exploring bespoke production techniques employing 3D laser sintering

programme.title: Peter Rice: An Engineer Imagines
programme.desc: A tribute to the life and ideas of Peter Rice, one of the most distinguished structural engineers of the late 20th century. The film traces his career from his native Ireland through London, Sydney and Paris, revealing how he pushed the boundaries of art and science, until his untimely death in 1992

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross captures a mountain at the height of winter, beneath one of his trademark cool blue skies

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Barra Best continues to explore lost railway routes in Northern Ireland, visiting lines that ran through towns and villages around Lough Neagh

After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: The life-saving secrets of the goats inhabiting America's Glacier National Park. Springtime in the wildlife reserve brings fresh challenges for the animals, who must learn to adapt to the treacherous wilderness if they are to survive to see the summer

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: Criss-crossing some of Northern Ireland's most spectacular countryside, Barra Best concludes his journey across the province by exploring Co Tyrone's lost railway routes

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross captures a glorious seascape, depicting the gentle rhythm of waves caressing a shoreline

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A farmer faces the loss of his savings as his livestock falls victim to a seemingly incurable disease, and James is powerless to help, while Tristan returns from school and admits to James that his exams may not have gone all that well

programme.title: Topkapi (1964)
programme.desc: International thieves mastermind the intricate robbery of an emerald dagger from Istanbul's Topkapi Palace Museum, and hire a small-time conman to drive the getaway car. Unfortunately, when the police find firearms in his vehicle, they coerce the hapless driver into spying for them. Crime comedy, starring Peter Ustinov in an Oscar-winning performance, with Melina Mercouri, Maximilian Schell, Robert Morley and Akim Tamiroff

programme.title: Parkinson
programme.desc: Highlights of the raconteur's appearances on the chat show, providing a fascinating insight into Ustinov's prolific career as writer, director, actor and ambassador for Unicef, and revealing his passion, intelligence and ability to entertain. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: A look at the hidden secrets of the universe

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank concludes his round-the-world trip with visits to some of Europe's greatest treasures, including the recently destroyed and rebuilt Mostar bridge, symbolically connecting the Christian and Muslim worlds, and the Parthenon in Athens. Italy figures heavily on his travels, as he explores Venice, Rome and Florence in search of marvels of the Roman Empire and the Renaissance - including the Pantheon and the chapel of the Medici princes

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: Performances by new wave bands from the BBC archives, including Elvis Costello, the Police, Ian Dury and the Blockheads, Squeeze, Blondie, Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers, the Cars, Patti Smith and Iggy Pop

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on November 29, with performances by artists including Dream Warriors, Dimples D, Chris Isaak, Pet Shop Boys, Soul II Soul featuring Kym Mazelle, Megabass, Patrick Macnee and Honor Blackman, Bombalurina featuring Timmy Mallet, Vanilla Ice and Deee-Lite

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the show's 1,400th edition, first aired on December 6 1990. With performances by Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood, the Farm, Cliff Richard, Madonna, MC Hammer, Snap, New Kids on the Block, Vanilla Ice and Betty Boo

programme.title: Duran Duran: There's Something You Should Know
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, John Taylor, Nick Rhodes and Roger Taylor open up about the highs and lows of the band's career - with archive footage of former guitarist Andy Taylor helping round out the story. The documentary charts their trajectory through seven of their 14 albums, each one uncovering a compelling chapter in the history of five lads from Birmingham who had one simple dream - to become the biggest band in the world. Contributors include Boy George, Nile Rodgers and Mark Ronson

programme.title: Duran Duran: A Night In
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, Nick Rhodes, John Taylor and Roger Taylor sit back to watch and talk through personally-selected clips of archive television, music shows, movies, performances, adverts and children's shows that have inspired them across their career. The band discuss their influences from the worlds of film and art, referencing Blade Runner, Andy Warhol and Tomorrow's World, and ranging from Barbarella to Virginia Plain and Midnight Express

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm

programme.title: Exotic Pop at the BBC

Cianna plays in the garden with her family, having a go at stacking colourful rings - but does she know what order they go in? Alexander heads out to play his very first game of mini golf. His club is as tall as he is! Meanwhile, Cianna and her brother have some splashing fun at a local park before collecting some leaves to do leaf printing at home. Suri begins by marinating the chicken in natural yoghurt, spring onions, turmeric powder and lemon juice. She then takes her special ingredient of saffron, grinds it down to a powder, and adds salt and natural yoghurt before she mixes it in with the rest of the marinade. Suri serves her chicken joojeh with tomatoes. They enjoy the meal while sitting on the floor, a traditional Iranian custom on special occasions. Will her friends like the special ingredient when it is cooked in her joojeh?

He solves a puzzle for Harry and Henry the Hedgehogs and encourages Brie the Bumblebee to get warm so she can fly. Brie is able to return the favour and points him in the right direction to meet Sid. programme.desc: Join 18-month-old Tiesha and Hudson as they discover the world around them and practice their coordination. Tiesha is building a tower out of blocks - how high will it go? Hudson is at home, doing water play with bath toys, splishing and splashing! At a ceramics shop, Tiesha paints her own ceramic using lots of colours. Hudson visits an aquarium, seeing fantastic fish through tunnels and pools. Hudson returns home, where he makes a porthole with fish! At the end of their day, Tiesha gets her pottery back, and Hudson plays a ball hitting game. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig

programme.title: At Home with Mr Tumble

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Diellza's family comes from Kosovo. She makes a traditional recipe of stuffed peppers. Diellza has lovely colourful ingredients for her stuffed peppers such as spring onions, sweetcorn, grated carrot. She also uses cooked rice, chopped tomatoes, parsley, garlic puree and paprika for seasoning. This yummy filling is stuffed into her peppers and placed in the oven to be cooked for her friends. To accompany her meal, Diellza serves her friends a traditional Kosovan drink made of yoghurt and water called kos. Will they enjoy her drink, and will they guess what the special ingredient is?

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

On their adventure with Mum and Dad, they discover colourful leaves, trees to climb, and bugs under rocks. But will they escape the treacherous troll hiding under the bridge? Back at home, there’s time for a warming cup of hot chocolate before settling down to bed.

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures

Looking for things to help them, Bitz finds some logs to roll the rock, but Bevel stops to eat a banana and the rock rolls off out of control, causing the logs to go everywhere. programme.desc: Icklewoo is fed up being called a little monster, so she pretends to be a big monster at school. But she soon learns being a grown up isn’t as fun as she thought it would be.

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘chicken’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see lots of chickens – a brown chicken, a white chicken, a clucking chicken and some friendly chickens. Dee meets a funky chicken, a hatching chicken and a cock-a-doodle-do-ing chicken! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Alara, Samaa, Reece, Eleanor, Ty’lana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is are some scented lavender bags, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are smelly. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate smelly objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The children’s nursery rhyme Ring A Ring O' Roses features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring smelly things too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Betty the Mouse’s little ones see a big shadow from their cosy nest in the potting shed, and all agree it must be a dinosaur. Word spreads around the garden and reaches Tilly the Tortoise, who is on a visit for the holidays. She can’t believe her luck – she loves dinosaurs! Tilly heads off on a search around the garden, bumping into Casey the Badger and Rosie the Rabbit, but no one has seen it. Auntie the Tawny Owl points her in the right direction – back to the potting shed! Will she ever find the dinosaur?

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a special walk through the town that starts in the daytime but finishes with a torch game in the dark. On the way they spot a spiky tree, have a close-up look at a daisy, light up their reflectors in the dark, and even make sounds like the sea!

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: We learn about objects that are colourful like a rainbow, from chameleons to kites, and explore the deeper meaning of rainbows. The episode concludes with a specially written poem about rainbow colours.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Anteater has a problem: his tongue is tied up in a knot!

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a magic coat rack and dancing toilets, and watch as Book Girl makes a music organ

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 19-month-old Jac and Jemima as they discover the world around them and start to learn how to care for themselves. Jac brushes his hair as he gets ready for the day, and Jemima chooses what she wants to wear. Once ready, Jac’s family visits a local farm shop where they choose a plant and get to pick vegetables! Meanwhile, Jemima goes to a play cafe, where she enjoys a tea party with mum and dad. Back at home, Jac and dad plant their new greenery and talk about how it’ll grow big and tall, just like Jac! Jemima finishes the day with painting flowers using her hands, but she must remember to wash them afterwards! Jac has his very first haircut - what will he think? Finally, after learning how to do so many new things, they both get ready for bed. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Baby Ben visits for the day, and Woolly shows Tig how helping her mum look after the child has its own rewards

programme.title: At Home with Mr Tumble
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is at home, blowing up balloons.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Disa's family comes from the Scandinavian country, Sweden. She is baking kardemummabullar, which is a Swedish recipe for cardamom buns. Disa makes a milk mixture, sweetened with honey, and mixes it into flour to make a dough. She then grinds her special ingredient of cardamom into a powder and adds some into the dough and the rest of it into brown sugar. The dough is flattened, the brown sugar and cardamom mix is spread onto the dough and folded in. The dough is then cut into strips and twisted and wrapped into a bun ready to be baked. Will her friends like the cardamom when it's baked in her kardemummabullar?

programme.desc: Amanpreet and her family are off on a animal adventure! They visit a safari park where they join the lions for a spot of lunch from the safety of their safari bus. She also gets to feed the giraffes, but will the gentle giants be tempted by her cabbage buffet?

programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch is invited to Djenne in Mali, Africa, to visit the people and to see one of his favourite things at work - mud. He is especially impressed by the spectacular Great Mosque of Djenne - the largest structure made from mud in the world

programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a semi-detached house just outside of Canterbury in Kent, and a mid-terrace in Wolverhampton. Martin Roberts visits a project in Crewe, Cheshire

programme.desc: CCTV footage reveals the truth when opportunists claim for injuries after being hit by a bus, and false claims of a serious injury sustained on a running machine are exposed

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out why astronaut Tim Peake's space capsule has landed in the city's 900-year-old cathedral

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover is asked to rescue a pastel portrait of a young couple damaged by water, while clockwork maestro Steve Fletcher gets to work on a 1950s toy rocket which used to make sparks. Finally, leather expert Suzie Fletcher works on a cherished purse that has seen better days, crumbling and being held together with elastic bands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Welsh Labour Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Sudden Death: My Sister's Silent Killer
programme.desc: A man sets out to learn more about the death of his 19-year-old sister from Sudden Adult Death Syndrome, a cause of cardiac death even the experts don't completely understand. In seeking answers, Patrick is shocked to discover the true extent of the problem, and that his sister Lauren was one of around 600 young and seemingly healthy people who die suddenly and unexpectedly from a Sudden Cardiac Death each year. He attends a bereavement counselling session with other young people who have lost a sibling to SADS, meets leading cardiac-pathologist Professor Mary Sheppard, and talks to former footballer Fabrice Muamba, who famously survived SADS after collapsing during a match

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam knows his vlog has little chance of going viral with such a small audience, so he visits several successful influencers in an attempt to identify his own appeal. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam must use his new-found status as a micro-influencer to become the face of an ethical campaign but ends up fronting an advert for an oil company. Meanwhile his vlogger pal Charlie South is being 'un-cancelled'.

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Question Time

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World

programme.title: Impossible
