Aside from houses, Margherita discovers the county's agricultural pedigree when she encounters some wonderfully preserved vintage tractors

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Cousins Joe and Alex, who have both experienced sight-loss problems, compete in the culinary contest. They both love to cook, and Alex inparticular has had to adjust to cooking by using some nifty kitchen gadgets, which he demonstrates to host Rylan Clark-Neal. The duo are paired up with chefs Mike Reid and Romy Gill

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Wood expert Will Kirk works wonders on an ornate, wired bird cage gifted to a daughter by the biological father she only managed to track down as an adult. Plus, leather expert Suzie Fletcher wrestles a beaten-up boxing bag back into shape

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Walking the Pembrokeshire coast, from Newport to Fishguard, weatherman Derek Brockway gets wet - from rain, an overturned kayak in the sea and from being in a hot-tub with a view. He also learns how the last invasion of Britain was foiled by a feisty woman. Archaeologist Dr Erin Lloyd-Jones explores the link between Stonehenge and the Preseli mountains and party-planner Onkar Singh Purewal visits a Hansel and Gretel-style house, covered in shells

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush takes drastic action, Martin faces a big decision and Lola confronts Isaac to ask why he took Patrick's phone. Tiffany overhears two girls talking about Keegan in the bar, having seen him online - but her confidence is shattered when they criticise his wife, prompting her to make a big blunder that leaves her in hot water

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and director Adrian Dunbar hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Katherine Ryan and Sunday Times journalist Tim Shipman joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian turns his hand to the intricate business of cake art, soon discovering that the devil is in the fiddly detail. A prestigious competition is on the horizon, offering the opportunity for Rhod's ambitious creation to wow the judges - or make people laugh for all the wrong reasons

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Welsh Liberal Democrats

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is challenged to live with a capsule wardrobe - selecting just 14 items from her vast collection of clothes and wearing nothing else for two weeks. She discovers what becomes of discarded clothes with a visit to a landfill site and investigates the work of online influencers in keeping the public hooked on buying the latest new look

programme.title: True Story (2015)
programme.desc: Disgraced New York Times journalist Michael Finkel discovers that Christian Longo, a man accused of murdering his family, has assumed his identity. Iintrigued, the reporter begins meeting with Longo, though as time goes on realises how dangerous his doppelganger really is. Jonah Hill, James Franco and Felicity Jones star in this thriller, based on Finkel's chilling, real-life story

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones chat to The Great British Sewing Bee judge Patrick Grant and the MasterChef contestants ahead of tonight's final

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a semi-detached house just outside of Canterbury in Kent, and a mid-terrace in Wolverhampton. Martin Roberts visits a project in Crewe, Cheshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage reveals the truth when opportunists claim for injuries after being hit by a bus, and false claims of a serious injury sustained on a running machine are exposed

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out why astronaut Tim Peake's space capsule has landed in the city's 900-year-old cathedral

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover is asked to rescue a pastel portrait of a young couple damaged by water, while clockwork maestro Steve Fletcher gets to work on a 1950s toy rocket which used to make sparks. Finally, leather expert Suzie Fletcher works on a cherished purse that has seen better days, crumbling and being held together with elastic bands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Scottish National Party

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Maggie battles to make senses of her returned memories while her daughters and Nicole try to mitigate the damage. Realising they'll soon be homeless, Scarlett steps in to fix Stevie's problems, while Cameron's poisonous words affect Dylan's worldview, and Rebecca's lies put her into a deeper hole

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Sudden Death: My Sister's Silent Killer
programme.desc: A man sets out to learn more about the death of his 19-year-old sister from Sudden Adult Death Syndrome, a cause of cardiac death even the experts don't completely understand. In seeking answers, Patrick is shocked to discover the true extent of the problem, and that his sister Lauren was one of around 600 young and seemingly healthy people who die suddenly and unexpectedly from a Sudden Cardiac Death each year. He attends a bereavement counselling session with other young people who have lost a sibling to SADS, meets leading cardiac-pathologist Professor Mary Sheppard, and talks to former footballer Fabrice Muamba, who famously survived SADS after collapsing during a match

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam knows his vlog has little chance of going viral with such a small audience, so he visits several successful influencers in an attempt to identify his own appeal. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam must use his new-found status as a micro-influencer to become the face of an ethical campaign but ends up fronting an advert for an oil company. Meanwhile his vlogger pal Charlie South is being 'un-cancelled'.

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons and David Farrell.

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond. Members of the public have an hour to put their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits Tentsmuir National Nature Reserve in Fife, to learn how the reserve has coped with the dramatic surge in visitors during lockdown, as local people reconnected with the nature on their doorstep. He also assesses how the farming industry is coping post-Brexit. Euan Mcilwraith looks back on the lessons learned as we mark the 20th anniversary of the foot-and-mouth outbreak, while in Sutherland Anne Lundon finds out what it was like to attend the remotest school in Britain. Arlene Stuart reports from the banks of the Forth, hoping to catch sight of a humpback whale called Barney

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. Guests including Richard Osman, Claudia Winkleman, Samantha Morton, AJ Odudu, Miles Jupp, Laura Whitmore, Lou Sanders, Chris McCausland, Sara Barron, Raj Bisram, Bob Mortimer, Sara Pascoe, Les Dennis, Mr Motivator, Stephen Hendry, Sindhu Vee, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Horne join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones chat to The Great British Sewing Bee judge Patrick Grant and the MasterChef contestants ahead of tonight's final

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a semi-detached house just outside of Canterbury in Kent, and a mid-terrace in Wolverhampton. Martin Roberts visits a project in Crewe, Cheshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage reveals the truth when opportunists claim for injuries after being hit by a bus, and false claims of a serious injury sustained on a running machine are exposed

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out why astronaut Tim Peake's space capsule has landed in the city's 900-year-old cathedral

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover is asked to rescue a pastel portrait of a young couple damaged by water, while clockwork maestro Steve Fletcher gets to work on a 1950s toy rocket which used to make sparks. Finally, leather expert Suzie Fletcher works on a cherished purse that has seen better days, crumbling and being held together with elastic bands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Suzie Lee's Home Cook Heroes
programme.desc: Suzie makes a selection of pork dishes, using the best quality Northern Irish meat sourced from County Down. She creates a Cantonese feast of crispy pork belly and sweet and succulent Char Sui pork, demonstrates how easy it is to make sausage rolls and finishes with a tangy upside down rhubarb and ginger cake

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: Dennis recalls his youth in Coalisland and rise on the professional snooker circuit. While Dennis often reached tournament finals, he always seemed to falter - leading the press to class him as an eternal runner-up. In 1985, however, everything changed with Dennis going on to make the ultimate sporting comeback in a World Championship final watched by a record-breaking 18.5 million people. He talks to Stephen Watson about his upbringing, the World Championship title win - and the origin of those famous upside-down glasses

programme.title: Sudden Death: My Sister's Silent Killer
programme.desc: A man sets out to learn more about the death of his 19-year-old sister from Sudden Adult Death Syndrome, a cause of cardiac death even the experts don't completely understand. In seeking answers, Patrick is shocked to discover the true extent of the problem, and that his sister Lauren was one of around 600 young and seemingly healthy people who die suddenly and unexpectedly from a Sudden Cardiac Death each year. He attends a bereavement counselling session with other young people who have lost a sibling to SADS, meets leading cardiac-pathologist Professor Mary Sheppard, and talks to former footballer Fabrice Muamba, who famously survived SADS after collapsing during a match

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam knows his vlog has little chance of going viral with such a small audience, so he visits several successful influencers in an attempt to identify his own appeal. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam must use his new-found status as a micro-influencer to become the face of an ethical campaign but ends up fronting an advert for an oil company. Meanwhile his vlogger pal Charlie South is being 'un-cancelled'.

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond, Amanda Holden and Danny Jones assist two contestants from Sheffield in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits the village of Send in Surrey to see a two-up two-down property with potential to extend. Martin Roberts is in Derby to visit a three-bed semi that he feels needs opening up to make the most of its space, while Martel Maxwell is in Co Durham in the village of Blackhill Colliery to see a two-bed end-of-terrace with a very low sounding guide price. All properties are auctioned and the hosts talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves to be the undoing of a con man claiming he cannot work due to an injury, and in America, a doctor abuses his position to commit medical health insurance fraud

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, experts Philip Serrell and Raj Bisram guide two teams around the Harrogate Fair, seeking keenly priced items to take to auction. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors progress and also stops off at one of best-preserved Victorian Turkish baths in the UK. Later at the auction, Caroline Hawley takes to the rostrum to wield the gavel

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and green-fingered siblings Harry and David Rich travel to Milton Keynes to compete for the chance to create a garden for husband and wife Vinay and Raunak. Vinay loves to host barbecues, so he's after a garden where he can do all that in style. Raunak, meanwhile, is more interested in filling the garden with vivid and dramatic colours, to match the style of decor she has used throughout their house. Both want the garden to represent the Hindu religion's belief in the power and importance of nature

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps an aspiring writer who wants to make a dramatic relocation from the frenetic bustle of east London to the rural idylls of Dumfries and Galloway in Scotland. With a budget of £300,000, he has his sights set on somewhere isolated that will inspire his creativity. Away from the houses, Alistair heads for the hills hoping to strike it lucky on a Scottish gold rush

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: As busy mothers, friends Lucy and Hannah want quick delicious dishes they can cook for all the family. They are in the right place today as chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis are both wizards in the kitchen and masters of fabulous food against the clock. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture experts, horologists, metal workers, ceramicists and upholsterers try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glory

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the live magazine show, featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The time comes for Kush to face the music at his plea hearing, and as he arrives at court he is anxious to see Ben, reminding him of what's at stake should he say the wrong thing. Sheree is exasperated with Patrick when he insists he wants to go ahead with the stroke trial. Tiffany and Dotty are amused at Keegan's popularity as he serves a long line of excited customers on his market stall

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A Scottish dog lover showcases a range of natural doggie treats, hoping Dragons Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies and Touker Suleyman will throw her a financial bone. Others braving the heat of the Den include a beauty entrepreneur with her range of affordable-yet-luxury waterless face oils and a Northumbrian inventor hoping to demonstrate his nifty construction bracket. Plus, a tech entrepreneur is put through her paces when she showcases fitness app aimed solely at women. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by reality star Spencer Matthews and model Vogue Williams, aiming to identify the real Mitch from a line-up of four. Mitch is a interiors enthusiast, who has lovingly created a green-heavy home in Hinckley near Leicester. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers reviews the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests and an alternative view on recent political headlines

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs a topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the virtual audience

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Documentary following the climate activist over the course of 2019 and 2020, as she travels the world exploring the science of global warming and challenging world leaders to take action on the growing crisis. The programme follows the impact of Covid-19 on her campaign, and the challenges of persuading a world living through one crisis to face up to the possibility of another on the horizon. In the first programme, Greta travels through North America on her way to a UN climate conference in Chile

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts reports from a derelict semi in Dartford, Kent, while Martel falls in love with a characterful terrace in Stoke-on-Trent. Lucy Alexander is at an old water booster station in Buckinghamshire, where there are grand plans to convert it into a family home

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A holiday insurance scam is foiled when investigators uncover forged documents, while CCTV footage exposes the false claims of a man who said he was hit by a bus

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, Sarah and Stewart are called to help a lady who has fallen at home, while in Glasgow, crew members rush to a woman struggling with breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques Fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets the lowdown on the resurgence of brown furniture, before the whole team heads to Derby for some auction action

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Fortunas of Bolton invite Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers to revamp their garden in Bolton. The final result needs to have lots to entertain the energetic boys, but that is not the only requirement - Luciano is from Sicily and would love a garden that reminds him of home, complete with a pizza oven

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Bedfordshire with a sociable couple who have £500,000 to find the right country home to suit them and their pooches and to house their very own bespoke garden pub. Aside from houses, Margherita discovers the county's agricultural pedigree when she encounters some wonderfully preserved vintage tractors

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Cousins Joe and Alex, who have both experienced sight-loss problems, compete in the culinary contest. They both love to cook, and Alex inparticular has had to adjust to cooking by using some nifty kitchen gadgets, which he demonstrates to host Rylan Clark-Neal. The duo are paired up with chefs Mike Reid and Romy Gill

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Wood expert Will Kirk works wonders on an ornate, wired bird cage gifted to a daughter by the biological father she only managed to track down as an adult. Plus, leather expert Suzie Fletcher wrestles a beaten-up boxing bag back into shape

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. Guests including Richard Osman, Claudia Winkleman, Samantha Morton, AJ Odudu, Miles Jupp, Laura Whitmore, Lou Sanders, Chris McCausland, Sara Barron, Raj Bisram, Bob Mortimer, Sara Pascoe, Les Dennis, Mr Motivator, Stephen Hendry, Sindhu Vee, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Horne join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush takes drastic action, Martin faces a big decision and Lola confronts Isaac to ask why he took Patrick's phone. Tiffany overhears two girls talking about Keegan in the bar, having seen him online - but her confidence is shattered when they criticise his wife, prompting her to make a big blunder that leaves her in hot water

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and director Adrian Dunbar hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Katherine Ryan and Sunday Times journalist Tim Shipman joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: True Story (2015)
programme.desc: Disgraced New York Times journalist Michael Finkel discovers that Christian Longo, a man accused of murdering his family, has assumed his identity. Iintrigued, the reporter begins meeting with Longo, though as time goes on realises how dangerous his doppelganger really is. Jonah Hill, James Franco and Felicity Jones star in this thriller, based on Finkel's chilling, real-life story

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team embark on a race against time to recapture a dangerous convict who has escaped from Bodmin prison - a case that leads them to an elaborate drugs racket. Starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Jimmy Yuill and Sean Chapman

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The young detective is suspended from duty following a recent case. However, he is roused from his bitterness and self-pity when the body of a woman who went missing at a funfair on Cowley Green is found near his home. He resolves to conduct a secret investigation of his own, which leads him to the city's social elite and a disturbing secret hidden in plain sight. Shaun Evans stars as the young Inspector Morse in the third series of the detective drama

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The withdrawal of a library book on the day of his death is the only clue to a student's murder. Two people who can be linked to the book give each other an alibi, and the police have their work cut out to identify who is lying. The killer eventually confesses and claims self-defence, forcing Phillips to face up to past demons, while Steel finds himself not only prosecuting the offender but also defending the victim. Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: For two years, Stephanie Blake has been tormented by a cyberstalker whose e-mails have grown more intimidating over time, but without an actual physical threat against her, there is little the team can do to help. Then, the case turns into a murder investigation, and one of the officers becomes a key witness for the prosecution, but the question arises as to how far he will go to bring the right man to justice. Guest starring Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective investigates a suspected kidnapping and gets his first lead when a ransom note is discovered. The missing woman then turns up dead at the wheel of a crashed car, smelling of alcohol. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A case from 13 years earlier returns to haunt Lewis when the evidence comes under review. Graham Lawrie was incarcerated in a secure psychiatric hospital after being found guilty of the murders of three police officers, but now the findings of the forensics lab have been called into question and an appeal has been launched. As Lawrie remains locked up ahead of his day in court, PC Mark Travis is lured to an isolated location with a hoax call and falls victim to a killer who uses the same weapon as that used in the original murders

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. They question a man seen in the area on the night of the killing, who protests his innocence and points the finger at a known gangster he claims was also at the scene. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks to the future following an attempt on his life, and while he weighs up his options, DCI Lane resolves to pursue her lofty ambitions. Meanwhile, DI Kersey investigates a salmon poacher's suspicious death. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy's mum becomes ever-more demanding. Mandy and Kelly plot to stop Mrs Kirk ruining Paddy's life. Graham decides to have an anti-drugs drive at school

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy tells Paddy the truth about his mother. Rachel cannot understand why anyone would plant drugs on her. Chris schemes to have Kathy all to himself

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is horrified when Frankie tells her Jon is blaming her for everything. Roy writes down his feelings for Hayley and reads them to her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Toyah is distraught that Spider has a girlfriend. Deirdre is upset to hear Ken will be testifying against her. Alma is horrified when Ken and Mike fight in the Rovers

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline finds out about Martin's imminent departure to London and confronts him over her uncertain future. Aunt Joan tries to solve her farm's money problems by opening a B&B - and ends up having to call the doctor for her first guest, local farmer Ted. Elsewhere, Bert organises a baby shower for Louisa. David Ross (The Green Green Grass) guest stars, with Martin Clunes, Stephanie Cole and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor spends his last day in Portwenn seeing an influx of patients as he prepares to close down the surgery and move to London. As he bids farewell to old friends and sets off to leave the village, he sees Louisa's teaching assistant Tasha fall from the Harbour Day stage and stops to help. But the bigger hold-up comes when pregnant Louisa is involved in a car accident. Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: High-court judge Rachel Callaghan (guest star Juliet Stevenson) is critically injured after being shot in the underground car park of her apartment. When it becomes clear a hitman was responsible, the team tries to discover who hired him. As evidence mounts against the accused, the judge's health deteriorates, and Castle is faced with a choice between friendship and respect for a person's right to die. Bill Paterson, Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Sophie, the wife of one of Kersey's friends, goes missing after an evening class and soon her tutor is found murdered. The detectives step in to investigate but the clues lead to standing stones linked to the entrance to hell in local superstition. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.desc: Marlon presses Biff to tell Kathy how he feels. Graham encourages Rachel to become a brunette. Mandy thinks Paddy would be better off with Jane

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is in for a shock when he finds out who Jean has enlisted to decorate Mike's bedroom

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector bans all the garage staff from smoking on duty - with dangerous consequences. Starring Reg Varney, Doris Hare, Anna Karen and Michael Robbins

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan overspends at the dog track and is unable to pay his housekeeping money, so hits on the idea of using the bus to give the senior citizens a day out in return for cash. Comedy, starring Reg Varney

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick finds himself drawn into a family feud after three children claim to have witnessed a supposed witch vandalising a funeral urn

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff takes his mood out on Roy and Marlon. Paddy's mum pleads with him to visit but he refuses. Rachel confronts Graham about the photo

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy is inconsolable about his mum. Mandy is convinced that Mrs Kirk is lying. Rachel wonders if she is a bad mother

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly grows weary of Spider's protests. Deirdre is in a dark place and hates herself for trusting Jon. Hayley tells Alma that she used to be a man

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally gets annoyed when Kevin objects to Chris babysitting. Spider and Emily resume their protest outside Firman's. Deirdre is stunned when Jon tracks her down

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team embark on a race against time to recapture a dangerous convict who has escaped from Bodmin prison - a case that leads them to an elaborate drugs racket. Starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Jimmy Yuill and Sean Chapman

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The young detective is suspended from duty following a recent case. However, he is roused from his bitterness and self-pity when the body of a woman who went missing at a funfair on Cowley Green is found near his home. He resolves to conduct a secret investigation of his own, which leads him to the city's social elite and a disturbing secret hidden in plain sight. Shaun Evans stars as the young Inspector Morse in the third series of the detective drama

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The withdrawal of a library book on the day of his death is the only clue to a student's murder. Two people who can be linked to the book give each other an alibi, and the police have their work cut out to identify who is lying. The killer eventually confesses and claims self-defence, forcing Phillips to face up to past demons, while Steel finds himself not only prosecuting the offender but also defending the victim. Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: For two years, Stephanie Blake has been tormented by a cyberstalker whose e-mails have grown more intimidating over time, but without an actual physical threat against her, there is little the team can do to help. Then, the case turns into a murder investigation, and one of the officers becomes a key witness for the prosecution, but the question arises as to how far he will go to bring the right man to justice. Guest starring Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman

programme.desc: Simon Templar becomes the target of a paid assassin and has to whittle down a list of potential avengers to find out who wants him killed. Time appears to not be on his side after a female journalist friend is abducted. Roger Moore stars, with Suzanne Lloyd

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The detective duo investigate a series of attacks on a giant corporation by a mysterious terrorist group, and uncover a shocking secret

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 uncovers a plan to hold the home secretary hostage in order to demand the release of a dangerous prisoner. Cowley takes the politician's place at an award ceremony, hoping that with Bodie as his bodyguard, he can draw the enemy into the open and thwart the attempt. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Gordon Jackson and Lewis Collins, and featuring Pamela Stephenson in an early role

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry arrives home to find a nine-year-old boy on his doorstep claiming to be his son, and ends up with a minding job he had not anticipated. He forms a bond with the youngster, eventually taking his side against his stepfather. Sharon Duce guest stars, with Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Monster Carp

programme.title: River Monsters

programme.title: Mr Bean

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson's clumsy alter ego returns to cause more chaos as he puts his dubious first aid skills to good use on an unconscious man in the street, makes a meal out of the simple task of posting a letter, and jets off for the holiday of a lifetime

programme.title: Rocky V (1990)

Drama sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Talia Shire and real-life boxer Tommy Morrison

programme.desc: Ray and Debra are shocked to realise they have virtually nothing to talk about except the kids, and resolve to take more of an interest in each other's hobbies

programme.desc: Ray is disturbed to learn Michael and Geoffrey are playing fairies in the school play, especially as they chose the roles themselves. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton, Sullivan Sweeten and Sawyer Sweeten

programme.title: Frasier

programme.desc: Daphne's mother infuriates Martin by meddling in his romantic affairs, while Frasier and Niles take part in a charity cycling event - even though neither of them knows how to ride a bike

Jean Johansson lines up five potential properties for their budget of £200,000, but will any of them seem the perfect place?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge's twin sisters Selma and Patty agree to stop smoking after they learn their father died of lung cancer. When Selma relapses after just 10 minutes, an angry Patty moves in with Marge and Homer. Meanwhile, Maggie goes on an adventure with some local creatures

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.desc: Martine and Grace both attend hospital appointments about lumps found in their breasts, before each of them have their interviews for the Businesswoman of the Year Award

First up is 32-year-old wrester Iain from Manchester, who loses his nerve when he's not in the ring. Can the show find hi the perfect partner to pin him down for good? programme.title: Trip Hazard: My Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Comedian Rosie Jones embarks on an adventure around Britain, gorging on culture, learning local traditions and witnessing unique sights. In this first episode, Rosie takes TV presenter Scarlett Moffatt to the Lake District. The duo visit the former home of William Wordsworth to seek inspiration for their own poetry, before re-enacting a Viking battle at Kendal Castle. They also manage to survive a driving course on a 100-year-old traction engine, meet a historian to learn about the region's ancient cuisine and run into a spot of bother with a very old sausage-making machine. Narrated by Olivia Colman, with Rachel Stubbings

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

Faye believes she has the weight of the entire restaurant on her shoulders, while Brenda feels she isn't treated as an equal in the business. With tensions rising, the food not up to scratch and everyone playing the blame game, the chef is faced with one of the trickiest situations he's ever encountered

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

They are followed by young couple Lloyd and Molly, who serve up a Thai menu, and bringing up the rear are Pete and Kirsty, with an evening themed around the letter P. programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp sews all the panels of her cushion together and creates some embellishments, from macrame feathers and old-fashioned pompoms to tassels that will set it apart from similar run-of-the-mill patchworks

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats a seven-month-old fox red Labrador puppy with lameness, caused by a joint defect called osteochondritis dissecans. He explains that medicine can be given, but in the end the dog may end up requiring a complete ankle replacement or a joint fusion on both sides. Jo has travelled 400 miles to bring her beloved four-year-old springer spaniel to see Noel. The pooch's kneecap keeps slipping in and out of her knee joint, and despite several previous procedures, the problems persist. programme.desc: Payment day finds the B&Bers ready to settle some scores. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Dan Jones examines Britain's most iconic canals, beginning with the 152-mile Grand Union Canal, which runs up the spine of the country. Constructed at the height of the Industrial Revolution, the task challenged even the greatest engineering minds of the time, including pioneers such as James Brindley, the man behind Britain's first commercial canal, and William Jessop, the person with whom responsibility lay for the building of the main link from Birmingham to London. Travelling along its route, Dan experiences first-hand the innovations behind its construction, including the Blisworth tunnel

programme.title: Police Interceptors

Also featured is security guard Michelle, whose drab, baggy clothes and reclusive lifestyle are a far cry from her fun, fashion-loving past

programme.title: Celebrities: What's Happened to Your Face?
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary taking a look at famous names who may have had cosmetic surgery. First under the microscope is music mogul Simon Cowell, one of TV's highest-paid stars. As well as his successful music career, Simon's face has been a constant source of speculation. While he's freely admitted to overdoing the Botox in the past and is estimated to have spent upward of £50,000 on his teeth alone, just how much other work he's had done is still hotly debated. Queen of Pop Madonna is also put under close scrutiny. As one of the most widely recognised women in the world, Madonna's found a way to stay relevant over the past four decades by constantly reinventing herself. But in her push to stay young, the superstar's changing face has become just as much of a talking point as her music

The excursion is rounded off with some traditional trappings, from ceilidhs and weaving to haggis-making

Plus, Jon laces up his walking boots to put the latest hiking tech through its paces with the help of Rambler's Association president and broadcaster Stuart Maconie

programme.title: World's Most Scenic River Journeys
programme.desc: A journey down the River Po, travelling across northern Italy from the snow-capped Alps to the spell-binding delta, where the river meets the Adriatic Sea. The trek concludes in Chioggia, an island town in the Venetian lagoon, home to one of the biggest fish markets on the Adriatic. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Churchill
programme.desc: In 1941, Winston Churchill arrived in the USA to be greeted like a movie star by an adoring public. Securing the support of US president Franklin Roosevelt and Soviet leader Joseph Stalin required all Churchill's skills as a communicator and negotiator. The supreme effort that he put in to keeping his allies on side almost killed him, but it gave him the chance to lead his country to victory. This episode also explores the Allies' extraordinary victory on D-Day

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs
programme.desc: Tony Blackburn narrates this study of 1986 pop hits, with series regulars Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini and Cheryl Baker joined by Sam Fox. Peter Gabriel's Sledgehammer, Sinitta's So Macho, Falco's Rock Me Amadeus, and the Bangles' Walk Like an Egyptian are some of the tracks being discussed

programme.title: The Story of the Songs
programme.desc: A look at three tracks that made Whitney Houston a star, with contributions by producers, songwriters, back-up singers, session musicians, choreographers, journalists and industry experts
